name: Buddy Tate
abstract: George Holmes "Buddy" Tate (February 22, 1913 – February 10, 2001) was an American jazz saxophonist and clarinetist.

interviews.title: Buddy Tate interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Galloway
about_quotes.evidence: Well Buddy I met for the first time when he was touring in Britain and he came to Glasgow in ‘58 and we met at a party afterwards.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Galloway interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: I think he replaced Buddy Tate at the time if I’m not mistaken.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: the first one that I ever did the band was Mel Lewis, Milt Hinton, Bucky Pizzarelli, Steve Kuhn, and Buddy Tate was the other saxophone player, and here we are playing somebody’s wedding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Thompson
about_quotes.evidence: we had a very warm friendship because he liked to play and practice, and I was always willing to practice and play with him. And we learned a lot from eachother.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I looked out of the wings and there is Buddy Tate and I said, 'How about getting your horn up,' which he did and came up and played a half hour with me

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I asked him if he would be interested to make a record with me for Pathe-Marconi... we made a nice album titled: 'Together at last'.
